title: Hajj Month
promptText: In which month do the main Hajj rites take place?
explanation: The pilgrimage is tied to the sacred final month of the Islamic year.

title: Ihram
answerText: The sacred state and dress entered before Hajj.
explanation: Pilgrims enter ihram before beginning the rites and avoid its restrictions.

title: Talbiyah
answerText: Labbayk Allahumma labbayk, the pilgrim's call of devotion.
explanation: It is a repeated declaration of readiness, sincerity, and worship.

title: Arafah
promptText: What is the core day of Hajj?
answerText: Standing at Arafah on 9 Dhul Hijjah.
explanation: The Day of Arafah is the heart of the pilgrimage.

title: Muzdalifah
promptText: What do pilgrims do after leaving Arafah?
answerText: Spend the night in Muzdalifah and collect pebbles.
explanation: Muzdalifah is a pause of rest, dhikr, and preparation for the next day.

title: Mina
promptText: Where do pilgrims stay and stone the Jamarat?
explanation: Mina becomes a temporary home for worship and movement between rites.

title: Jamarat
answerText: Stoning the pillars that symbolize rejecting Satan.
explanation: It recalls Ibrahim rejecting temptation and staying firm in obedience.

title: Tawaf
explanation: It is one of the central acts of pilgrimage around the House of Allah.

title: Sa'i
explanation: Sa'i remembers Hajar's search for water and Allah's provision.

title: Sacrifice
promptText: What rite follows on the 10th of Dhul Hijjah for many pilgrims?
answerText: The sacrifice, called hady or udhiyah.
explanation: This rite links Hajj with gratitude, feeding others, and remembrance of Allah.

title: Ifadah
promptText: Match each Hajj rite to what happens there.
explanation: It is one of the essential tawafs that complete the pilgrimage.

title: Farewell Tawaf
promptText: Tap these Hajj moments in the correct travel order.
answerText: Ihram, Arafah, Muzdalifah, Mina, Farewell Tawaf.
explanation: The farewell tawaf closes the journey before departure.
